jokes.text: Lil' Jon's medical exam where he can only say 'Yeah!', 'What?!', 'Okay!' - responding to pain tests and insurance questions
jokes.setup: Standard medical examination with yes/no questions
jokes.punchline: Lil' Jon's extremely limited vocabulary forces absurd responses to medical questions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - could only work with Lil' Jon. The escalating frustration of the insurance questions creates sustained comedy.

jokes.text: Escalating confusion over 'proof of insurance' with rapid-fire 'What?!' exchanges
jokes.setup: Doctor asks for proof of insurance
jokes.punchline: Both characters get stuck in an infinite loop of 'What?!' responses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, but the premise is slightly predictable given the character's established speech pattern.

jokes.text: Lil' Jon's drug confession: 'Cocaine? Absolutely not. Marijuana? Yeah! Yeah, yeah!'
jokes.setup: Doctor asking about recreational drug use, expecting denial
jokes.punchline: Lil' Jon enthusiastically admits to marijuana use while denying cocaine
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and character consistency. The selective honesty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Lil' Jon admits to smoking marijuana with enthusiastic 'Yeah!' responses
jokes.setup: Doctor asks about recreational drug use, Lil' Jon denies cocaine and other drugs
jokes.punchline: When asked about marijuana, he enthusiastically confirms with multiple 'Yeah!' responses
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment but fairly predictable given rap culture associations.

jokes.text: Dave's anti-weed PSA parody commercial showing extreme paranoia and violence over a cheeseburger
jokes.setup: Dave introduces quitting smoking because commercials work on him
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous escalation from burger confusion to murder and body disposal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of PSA format with absurd escalation. Dark humor executed well.

jokes.text: Dave announces he's quit smoking weed but audience laughs skeptically
jokes.setup: Dave seriously announces he's quit smoking marijuana
jokes.punchline: Audience immediately laughs in disbelief, forcing Dave to insist 'No, I'm serious this time'
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/payoff with audience reaction. Standard stand-up crowd work.

jokes.text: Dave: 'I just love the fact that I have a show where we can run over a kid and everyone just busts out laughing. Only this show.'
jokes.setup: Acknowledging the extreme content of the previous sketch
jokes.punchline: Celebrating the show's ability to make murder comedy acceptable
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary with excellent economy. Acknowledges the show's transgressive nature.

jokes.text: Paul Mooney reviewing 'Gone With The Wind': 'I thought Scarlett was a ho, because she went bed with everybody, uh, but Mammy.'
jokes.setup: Traditional movie review of classic film
jokes.punchline: Mooney's blunt, sexually frank take on the romanticized protagonist
jokes.explanation: Subverts reverent film criticism with crude honesty. Perfect character voice for Mooney.

jokes.text: Mooney calls Scarlett O'Hara a ho because 'she went to bed with everybody but Mammy'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Scarlett O'Hara as romantic heroine
jokes.punchline: Mooney's crude but accurate assessment of her promiscuity
jokes.explanation: Classic Mooney - taking a blunt, working-class perspective on high culture.

jokes.text: Mooney's Mammy fantasy: 'the best scene in the movie is when Mammy told the white people, get off my porch, white trash! I stood and I applauded.'
jokes.setup: Discussing favorite scene from Gone With The Wind
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated scene where the black character has power
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion creating wish-fulfillment that never existed. High originality and character integration.

jokes.text: Mooney on Hattie McDaniel: 'She came back as Oprah Winfrey to get her money.'
jokes.setup: Discussing how Hattie McDaniel was excluded from Gone With The Wind premiere
jokes.punchline: Supernatural reincarnation theory connecting old and new black media figures
jokes.explanation: Incredibly original connection with perfect economy. Brilliant pop culture observation that feels profound.

jokes.text: Mooney on barbershops: 'You know it's just a front. It really... they really sell drugs out of barbershops.'
jokes.setup: Host giving earnest review of Barbershop movie
jokes.punchline: Mooney casually stating criminal conspiracy as fact
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan delivery but relies on familiar stereotype. Decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Mooney suggests barbershops are drug fronts
jokes.setup: Reviewer describes Barbershop as wholesome comedy about Chicago barbershop
jokes.punchline: Mooney matter-of-factly states they really sell drugs
jokes.explanation: Relies on familiar stereotype. Expected from Mooney's character but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Mooney's movie pitch: 'The Last Nigger On Earth starring Tom Hanks.'
jokes.setup: Criticizing Hollywood's casting of white actors in culturally specific roles
jokes.punchline: Proposing the ultimate reversal with America's most wholesome actor
jokes.explanation: Peak comedy writing. Perfect structure, maximum impact, completely original concept. The Tom Hanks casting makes it transcendent.

jokes.text: Dave claims ESPN shows 'World Series of Dice' as black gambling
jokes.setup: Poker gets mainstream ESPN coverage
jokes.punchline: Black gambling gets represented by street dice games
jokes.explanation: Good premise that exposes cultural double standards. Sets up the elaborate sketch perfectly.

jokes.text: Announcers ask why they're at Marcy Projects - 'To buy weed?' 'Not this time, Bill'
jokes.setup: Setting up location at housing projects
jokes.punchline: First assumption is drug dealing, quickly corrected
jokes.explanation: Plays on stereotypes but in service of larger parody. Gets the joke quickly without belaboring it.

jokes.text: Phyuck Yiu introduction and name pronunciation
jokes.setup: Introducing Asian dice player from the Far East
jokes.punchline: Name that sounds like 'Fuck You' when pronounced
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay that relies on pronunciation gag. Cheap but effective for the character introduction.

jokes.text: Phyuck Yiu: 'Konichiwa, bitches!'
jokes.setup: Formal Japanese greeting expected
jokes.punchline: Adding inappropriate American slang to traditional greeting
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of formal and street language. Efficient and quotable character moment.

jokes.text: Leonard Washington's introduction: 'Where I'm from? A little town called, none of your goddamned business.'
jokes.setup: Standard athlete introduction format
jokes.punchline: Hostile non-answer that subverts expectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment with excellent misdirection. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Visual gag of Ashy Larry's extremely ashy skin
jokes.setup: Announcer asking why they call him Ashy Larry
jokes.punchline: Extreme visual of his dry, ashy appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual character comedy. The extreme commitment to the visual gag makes it memorable.

jokes.text: 'That man is drier than Kunta Kinte's ankle.'
jokes.setup: Describing Ashy Larry's dry skin condition
jokes.punchline: Historical reference to slavery connecting dryness to suffering
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference with great economy. Dark humor that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Ashy Larry: 'I stole this money outta my girl purse. She think I'm out emptying the trash.'
jokes.setup: Player explaining their stake in the game
jokes.punchline: Admitting theft from girlfriend while maintaining domestic deception
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Larry's desperate circumstances and poor decision-making.

jokes.text: Ashy Larry: 'All right, but I'm about to go from Ashy to classy.'
jokes.setup: Being insulted about his appearance before rolling dice
jokes.punchline: Rhyming wordplay showing misplaced confidence
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with good character voice. Simple but effective and memorable.

jokes.text: Grits n' Gravy on spending his winnings: 'I bought my momma a car and I spent the rest on PCP.'
jokes.setup: Expected wholesome use of gambling winnings
jokes.punchline: Sweet gesture immediately followed by drug admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition with excellent economy. The wholesome/depraved contrast is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Commentator: 'Loves his mother. Loves PCP.'
jokes.setup: Grits' confession about spending his money
jokes.punchline: Treating both loves as equally valid character traits
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan callback with maximum economy. The equal treatment of both traits is brilliant.

jokes.text: Leonard: 'I got babies to kiss and bitches to catch.'
jokes.setup: Leonard explaining why he needs to speed up the game
jokes.punchline: Reversing the politician cliché 'babies to kiss and hands to shake'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay subverting political cliché. Perfect for Leonard's character voice.

jokes.text: Grits elimination line: 'And when I leave, come together like butt cheeks.'
jokes.setup: Grits being eliminated from the game
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor for unity using anatomical reference
jokes.explanation: Weird and memorable character moment. The absurdist metaphor fits the character's drug-addled personality.

jokes.text: Robber: 'I want everybody in here to get butt-ass naked!'
jokes.setup: Standard robbery interruption of the dice game
jokes.punchline: Unusual robbery demand for complete nudity
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation that subverts typical robbery scenarios. The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Commentator: 'dice games get robbed. It happens all the time. They very rarely come to a reasonable end.'
jokes.setup: Robbery interrupting the televised dice game
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact acceptance of criminal activity as normal
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery treating chaos as routine. Good observational humor about street gambling reality.

jokes.text: Leonard: 'I'm Leonard Washington, I don't get butt-naked for nobody.'
jokes.setup: Robber demanding everyone get naked
jokes.punchline: Leonard's stubborn refusal based on personal dignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency. Leonard's dignity in absurd situation creates great character comedy.

jokes.text: Commentator after Leonard gets shot: 'you noticed he shot him below the waist, so it's not attempted murder. That was very intelligent on Quills part. Quills knows the law.'
jokes.setup: Leonard being shot during robbery
jokes.punchline: Analyzing criminal strategy with sports commentary detachment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor treating violence as strategic sports play. The legal analysis is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Announcer notes Quills shot below waist 'so it's not attempted murder. Quills knows the law'
jokes.setup: Leonard getting shot during robbery
jokes.punchline: Professional sports commentary on criminal law strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. Treating criminal strategy with sports analysis tone is perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: Ashy Larry returns asking 'Where the dice? Where the dice?' during the robbery
jokes.setup: Violent robbery in progress
jokes.punchline: Larry completely oblivious to danger, only focused on gambling
jokes.explanation: Perfect character callback showing Larry's single-minded focus. Good comedic timing.

jokes.text: Robber to Larry: 'Go upstairs and put on some lotion, right now!'
jokes.setup: Robber dealing with various victims
jokes.punchline: Even the robber is disturbed by Larry's ashy condition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that escalates the running gag. Even criminals have standards about hygiene.

jokes.text: Commentator: 'I saw this robbery coming from a mile away, which is why I put my car keys up my ass.'
jokes.setup: Explaining preparedness for the robbery
jokes.punchline: Extremely uncomfortable hiding place presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with perfect deadpan delivery. The extreme preparation is hilariously specific.

jokes.text: Dave: 'You think I'm this nice in real life? Fuck that, son! It's because I'm on TV. I'm wild! I pull my balls out right now, I don't care.'
jokes.setup: Explaining the difference between TV persona and real personality
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd threats of public indecency
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary with escalating absurdity. Shows Dave's willingness to break fourth wall.

jokes.text: Vernon's office outburst: 'Get your motherfucking hand out of my face. You heard me, motherfucker... Do you want a little soft shoe? Should I juggle some watermelons for you, boss?'
jokes.setup: Awkward high-five attempt from white mentor
jokes.punchline: Explosive overreaction referencing minstrel show stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing racial sensitivity explosion. The historical references add depth to the outburst.

jokes.text: Vernon: 'Allow me reintroduce myself, my name is Homes... I used to beat motherfuckers up just like you, just for walking around my way! Wu-Tang!'
jokes.setup: Vernon's corporate persona cracking
jokes.punchline: Full transformation into street persona with random hip-hop references
jokes.explanation: Good character transformation showing code-switching extreme. The random Wu-Tang reference adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Vernon's current situation: gas station job making $6.45 an hour, stinking of gasoline
jokes.setup: Vernon's former success as corporate VP
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal to minimum wage manual labor
jokes.explanation: Effective ironic payoff showing consequences. The specific details make the fall more concrete.

jokes.text: Vernon: 'A dollar? What am I gonna get with a dollar, nigga? I got kids, and that's real. Wu-Tang!'
jokes.setup: Receiving small tip at gas station
jokes.punchline: Still maintaining aggressive persona while asking legitimate question
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Vernon still stuck in his adopted persona despite consequences.
